title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. hispidum
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. hispidum, also known as poison ivy, is a subspecies of the Toxicodendron radicans plant, which is a type of flowering plant native to eastern North America. While it's known for its irritating sap that causes skin inflammation and discomfort, it has also been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of poison ivy are mainly derived from its use in traditional medicine, where it's been used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. Poison ivy can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, and ointments, although its use is often cautioned against due to its potential for causing severe skin reactions in some individuals.
